description:
You are a Kubernetes platform operator who needs to generate a quick inventory of the live manifests you have saved on-disk.

    Files that already exist:
      1. /home/user/k8s/manifests/deployment-web.yaml
      2. /home/user/k8s/manifests/deployment-api.yaml
      3. /home/user/k8s/manifests/statefulset-db.yaml

    Each YAML file starts with a single comment line that looks like  
        # filename:<exact-file-name>  
    followed by an ordinary Kubernetes manifest.  The three concrete files that are present before you start are exactly the ones listed above; no sub-directories exist inside /home/user/k8s/manifests.

    Your tasks:

    1. Create a directory /home/user/k8s/summary if it does not yet exist.
    2. Inside that directory create a *tab-separated* file called manifest_summary.tsv that contains exactly four lines in the following canonical order:
           • Line-1 is a header line that must literally be:  
                 kind<TAB>name<TAB>replicas
           • Line-2 describes the manifest whose metadata.name is api-server
           • Line-3 describes the manifest whose metadata.name is db-storage
           • Line-4 describes the manifest whose metadata.name is web-frontend

       Each data line must have three TAB-separated columns in this order:
           kind<TAB>metadata.name<TAB>spec.replicas

       Example shape (← TAB characters, **not spaces**):
           kind←name←replicas
           Deployment←api-server←2
           StatefulSet←db-storage←1
           Deployment←web-frontend←3

       Make sure the file ends with a single trailing newline (i.e., the cursor would be on the next blank line if you opened it in vim).
    3. Create a directory /home/user/k8s/logs if it does not exist and write a plain-text file extraction.log inside it.  
       The log must have exactly three lines, one per source manifest, in *alphabetical order by filename*.  
       The format of every line must be:  
            <filename>: processed  
       where <filename> is the basename of the manifest file that was handled.  
       End the log with a trailing newline as well.

    Strict output requirements that the automated checker will enforce:
       • manifest_summary.tsv exists at the exact path /home/user/k8s/summary/manifest_summary.tsv  
       • It contains exactly four lines and three TAB-separated columns per line.  
       • The header line is exactly: kind<TAB>name<TAB>replicas  
       • Data lines are ordered by the **name** column alphabetically.  
       • extraction.log exists at /home/user/k8s/logs/extraction.log  
       • extraction.log contains exactly three lines, alphabetically sorted by the manifest *file names*, each ending with “: processed”.

    Hints you might consider (but do not reveal your whole solution here):
       • Use grep / awk / cut pipelines, or yq if you prefer.  
       • Remember that you can sort by the second field of a stream.  
       • The manifest files are small; feel free to cat them in a loop.  
       • Ensure directories exist before writing.  
       • Be careful to output TABs, not spaces; “echo -e” or printf can help.

    When you are finished, you should be able to run:
         cat -A /home/user/k8s/summary/manifest_summary.tsv
    and see the TABs printed as “^I” and exactly the four lines described above.
truth:
Pre-existing files and their exact contents (with a trailing newline on the last line of each file):

    /home/user/k8s/manifests/deployment-web.yaml
    ---------------------------------------------------
    # filename:deployment-web.yaml
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    metadata:
      name: web-frontend
    spec:
      replicas: 3
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: web
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: nginx
            image: nginx:1.21
    ---------------------------------------------------

    /home/user/k8s/manifests/deployment-api.yaml
    ---------------------------------------------------
    # filename:deployment-api.yaml
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    metadata:
      name: api-server
    spec:
      replicas: 2
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: api
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: server
            image: my-api:2.0
    ---------------------------------------------------

    /home/user/k8s/manifests/statefulset-db.yaml
    ---------------------------------------------------
    # filename:statefulset-db.yaml
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: StatefulSet
    metadata:
      name: db-storage
    spec:
      replicas: 1
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: db
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: postgres
            image: postgres:13
    ---------------------------------------------------

    Expected artefacts AFTER the agent completes the task:

    1. /home/user/k8s/summary/manifest_summary.tsv  
       Content (each “→” represents a literal TAB character, not part of the file):

            kind→name→replicas
            Deployment→api-server→2
            StatefulSet→db-storage→1
            Deployment→web-frontend→3

       followed by a single trailing newline.

    2. /home/user/k8s/logs/extraction.log  
       Content:

            deployment-api.yaml: processed
            deployment-web.yaml: processed
            statefulset-db.yaml: processed

       followed by a single trailing newline.

    Permissions: the whole /home/user/k8s tree is owned by the normal user account running the task and writable by that user.
